description: coder-workflow comprehensive hooks — skill triggers (first), session lifecycle, safety guards, graph maintenance, task tracking, change awareness, and session telemetry.

hooks.command: bash

bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); PROMPT=$(jq -r ".prompt // empty" <<< "$INPUT" 2>/dev/null | head -c 140); mkdir -p .claude 2>/dev/null || true; printf "[%s] PROMPT: %s\n" "$(date -u +%T)" "$PROMPT" >> .claude/session-$(date +%Y%m%d).log 2>/dev/null; exit 0'
bash

bash -c 'DB=.codegraph/graph.db; LOCK=.codegraph/scan.lock; if [ ! -f "$DB" ]; then touch "$LOCK" 2>/dev/null || true; (command -v coder-workflow >/dev/null 2>&1 && coder-workflow scan 2>/dev/null || npx codegraph-mapper scan 2>/dev/null || true); rm -f "$LOCK" 2>/dev/null || true; fi; exit 0'
bash -c 'exit 0'
bash
echo 'Context was compacted and then resumed. Skills, orchestrator, and CodeGraph MCP tools are still active. Re-read your active task before proceeding.'
echo 'Session cleared. Clean slate — use /coder-workflow:coder-orchestrator for any coding task.'
bash
bash

bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); CMD=$(jq -r ".tool_input.command // empty" <<< "$INPUT" 2>/dev/null); if printf "%s" "$CMD" | grep -qE "git\s+reset\s+--hard|git\s+clean\s+-[a-zA-Z]*f"; then jq -n "{systemMessage: \"coder-workflow notice: git reset --hard or git clean -f will destroy uncommitted work. Ensure nothing unsaved exists before proceeding.\"}"; fi; exit 0'

bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); CMD=$(jq -r ".tool_input.command // empty" <<< "$INPUT" 2>/dev/null); if printf "%s" "$CMD" | grep -qE "DROP\s+(TABLE|DATABASE|SCHEMA)|TRUNCATE\s+TABLE"; then jq -n "{systemMessage: \"coder-workflow notice: Destructive SQL detected (DROP/TRUNCATE). Confirm this is targeting a dev/test database, not production.\"}" ; fi; exit 0'

bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); CMD=$(jq -r ".tool_input.command // empty" <<< "$INPUT" 2>/dev/null); if printf "%s" "$CMD" | grep -qE "git\s+merge\s+"; then jq -n "{systemMessage: \"coder-workflow notice: git merge detected. If this involves parallel worktree changes, verify no conflicts before completing. Run git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U after merge to check.\"}"; fi; exit 0'
bash
bash -c 'exit 0'
bash -c 'exit 0'
bash -c 'exit 0'

bash -c 'jq -n "{systemMessage: \"coder-workflow: You are in an Explore context. You MUST use mcp__codegraph__query_graph, mcp__codegraph__search_code, mcp__codegraph__analyze_impact, or mcp__codegraph__summarize_architecture to explore the codebase. Do NOT use bash find/grep unless graph tools fail.\"}"'

bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); AGENT=$(jq -r ".tool_input.subagent_type // .tool_input.Subagents[0].TypeName // empty" <<< "$INPUT" 2>/dev/null); if echo "$AGENT" | grep -qi "explore"; then jq -n "{systemMessage: \"coder-workflow: The Explore agent MUST prioritize mcp__codegraph tools (query_graph, search_code, analyze_impact). Avoid bash find/grep.\"}"; fi; exit 0'

bash -c 'jq -n "{hookSpecificOutput: {hookEventName: \"PostToolUse\", additionalContext: \"coder-workflow: Impact Radius active — bugs found within your modified files must be tracked and fixed. Ignore pre-existing debt outside your FILE_MANIFEST.\"}}"'

bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); FILE=$(jq -r ".tool_input.file_path // .tool_input.path // empty" <<< "$INPUT" 2>/dev/null); mkdir -p .claude 2>/dev/null || true; printf "[%s] WRITE: %s\n" "$(date -u +%T)" "$FILE" >> .claude/session-$(date +%Y%m%d).log 2>/dev/null; exit 0'

bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); CMD=$(jq -r ".tool_input.command // empty" <<< "$INPUT" 2>/dev/null); if printf "%s" "$CMD" | grep -qE "(npm (i|install|ci)|yarn add|bun add|pnpm add)"; then echo "Package install detected — verify lock file is committed and no phantom dependencies were introduced."; fi; exit 0'
hooks.if: Bash(npm install *)|Bash(npm i *)|Bash(npm ci)|Bash(yarn add *)|Bash(bun add *)|Bash(pnpm add *)

hooks.command: bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); CMD=$(jq -r ".tool_input.command // empty" <<< "$INPUT" 2>/dev/null); mkdir -p .claude 2>/dev/null || true; printf "[%s] GIT COMMIT: %s\n" "$(date -u +%T)" "$CMD" >> .claude/session-$(date +%Y%m%d).log 2>/dev/null; exit 0'

bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); CMD=$(jq -r ".tool_input.command // empty" <<< "$INPUT" 2>/dev/null); OUTPUT=$(jq -r ".tool_response // .output // empty" <<< "$INPUT" 2>/dev/null | head -c 300); if printf "%s" "$CMD" | grep -qE "npm (test|run test|run lint|run typecheck|run check)"; then mkdir -p .claude 2>/dev/null || true; printf "[%s] TEST/LINT: %s\n" "$(date -u +%T)" "$CMD" >> .claude/session-$(date +%Y%m%d).log 2>/dev/null; fi; exit 0'
hooks.if: Bash(npm test)|Bash(npm run test *)|Bash(npm run lint *)|Bash(npm run typecheck)

hooks.command: bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); CMD=$(jq -r ".tool_input.command // empty" <<< "$INPUT" 2>/dev/null); if printf "%s" "$CMD" | grep -qE "git\s+checkout|git\s+switch"; then BRANCH=$(printf "%s" "$CMD" | grep -oE "[a-zA-Z0-9_.-]+" | tail -1); mkdir -p .claude 2>/dev/null || true; printf "[%s] BRANCH SWITCH: %s\n" "$(date -u +%T)" "$BRANCH" >> .claude/session-$(date +%Y%m%d).log 2>/dev/null; fi; exit 0'

bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); TOOL=$(jq -r ".tool_name // empty" <<< "$INPUT" 2>/dev/null); mkdir -p .claude 2>/dev/null || true; printf "[%s] GRAPH: %s\n" "$(date -u +%T)" "$TOOL" >> .claude/session-$(date +%Y%m%d).log 2>/dev/null; exit 0'

bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); AGENT=$(jq -r ".tool_input.subagent_type // .tool_input.prompt // empty" <<< "$INPUT" 2>/dev/null | head -c 100); mkdir -p .claude 2>/dev/null || true; printf "[%s] AGENT SPAWN: %s\n" "$(date -u +%T)" "$AGENT" >> .claude/session-$(date +%Y%m%d).log 2>/dev/null; exit 0'

bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); TOOL=$(jq -r ".tool_name // empty" <<< "$INPUT" 2>/dev/null); ERR=$(jq -r ".tool_error // .error // empty" <<< "$INPUT" 2>/dev/null | head -c 200); mkdir -p .claude 2>/dev/null || true; printf "[%s] FAIL [%s]: %s\n" "$(date -u +%T)" "$TOOL" "$ERR" >> .claude/session-$(date +%Y%m%d).log 2>/dev/null; exit 0'

bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); COUNT=$(jq ".tool_results | length // 0" <<< "$INPUT" 2>/dev/null || echo "?"); printf "[%s] BATCH: %s tool calls resolved\n" "$(date -u +%T)" "$COUNT" >> /tmp/cw-session.log 2>/dev/null; exit 0'
echo 'Turn complete. Mandatory checklist before next user message: (1) all tasks tracked, (2) no bugs left unfixed WITHIN your Impact Radius, (3) targeted tests pass, (4) graph is current.'

bash -c '
rm -f /tmp/cw-grep-warned-$$ 2>/dev/null || true
LOG=.claude/session-$(date +%Y%m%d).log
mkdir -p .claude 2>/dev/null || true
if test -f .codegraph/graph.db; then
  UPDATE_OUTPUT=$(command -v coder-workflow >/dev/null 2>&1 && coder-workflow update 2>&1 || npx codegraph-mapper update 2>&1 || true)
  if echo "$UPDATE_OUTPUT" | grep -qiE "error|fail|exception"; then
    printf "[%s] GRAPH UPDATE FAILED: %s\n" "$(date -u +%T)" "$UPDATE_OUTPUT" >> "$LOG" 2>/dev/null || true
  fi
fi
exit 0'
echo 'Rate limited. Wait 60+ seconds before retrying. Consider breaking the work into smaller incremental steps to reduce per-turn token load.'
echo 'Output token limit hit. Break the response into smaller steps — ask for one piece at a time rather than generating everything at once.'

bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); ERR=$(jq -r ".error_type // unknown" <<< "$INPUT" 2>/dev/null); printf "[%s] STOP FAILURE: %s\n" "$(date -u +%T)" "$ERR" >> /tmp/cw-session.log 2>/dev/null; echo "API error ($ERR) — session ended unexpectedly. Resume with /resume and re-read task state before continuing."; exit 0'
echo 'Package manifest changed on disk. Run the appropriate install command before using any new or updated packages.'
echo 'Env file changed on disk. Verify it is gitignored and contains no committed secrets before the next git operation.'
echo 'CLAUDE.md changed on disk — project instructions updated. New rules apply to subsequent turns immediately.'
echo 'hooks.json changed on disk — hook configuration updated. Changes take effect on the next hook event.'
echo 'TypeScript/linter config changed on disk. A rebuild may be needed: npm run build && npm run typecheck'
echo 'MCP configuration changed on disk. Restart Claude Code (or reload plugins) for the new server configuration to take effect.'
echo 'Agent definition changed on disk. New agent instructions apply to subsequent subagent spawns.'
echo 'Skill definition changed on disk. New skill rules apply immediately — invoke the skill to pick up updated behavior.'

bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); CWD=$(jq -r ".cwd // empty" <<< "$INPUT" 2>/dev/null); [ -z "$CWD" ] && CWD=$(pwd); echo "cwd changed to: $CWD"; if [ ! -f "$CWD/.codegraph/graph.db" ]; then echo "  No CodeGraph in this directory. Run mcp__codegraph__scan_codebase if code analysis is needed."; fi; exit 0'
echo 'Context compacted. All skills, orchestrator, and graph MCP tools remain active. Re-read active task state before your next action — the CodeGraph DB is unchanged.'

bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); AGENT=$(jq -r ".agent_type // .subagent_type // unknown" <<< "$INPUT" 2>/dev/null); mkdir -p .claude 2>/dev/null || true; printf "[%s] AGENT START: %s\n" "$(date -u +%T)" "$AGENT" >> .claude/session-$(date +%Y%m%d).log 2>/dev/null; LOCK=.claude/agent-depth.lock; ( flock -x 200; DEPTH=0; [ -f "$LOCK" ] && DEPTH=$(cat "$LOCK" 2>/dev/null | grep -E "^[0-9]+$" || echo 0); printf "%s" "$((DEPTH+1))" > "$LOCK" ) 200>.claude/agent-depth.lock.flock; exit 0'

bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); AGENT=$(jq -r ".agent_type // .subagent_type // unknown" <<< "$INPUT" 2>/dev/null); mkdir -p .claude 2>/dev/null || true; printf "[%s] AGENT STOP: %s\n" "$(date -u +%T)" "$AGENT" >> .claude/session-$(date +%Y%m%d).log 2>/dev/null; LOCK=.claude/agent-depth.lock; ( flock -x 200; if [ -f "$LOCK" ]; then DEPTH=$(cat "$LOCK" 2>/dev/null | grep -E "^[0-9]+$" || echo 1); NEW=$(( DEPTH - 1 )); if [ "$NEW" -le 0 ]; then rm -f "$LOCK"; else printf "%s" "$NEW" > "$LOCK"; fi; fi ) 200>.claude/agent-depth.lock.flock; exit 0'
bash

bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); TITLE=$(jq -r ".task.title // .title // unknown" <<< "$INPUT" 2>/dev/null); mkdir -p .claude 2>/dev/null || true; printf "[%s] TASK done: %s\n" "$(date -u +%T)" "$TITLE" >> .claude/session-$(date +%Y%m%d).log 2>/dev/null; echo "Task complete: $TITLE"; exit 0'

bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); FILE=$(jq -r ".file // .path // unknown" <<< "$INPUT" 2>/dev/null); REASON=$(jq -r ".reason // unknown" <<< "$INPUT" 2>/dev/null); printf "[%s] INSTRUCTIONS [%s]: %s\n" "$(date -u +%T)" "$REASON" "$FILE" >> /tmp/cw-session.log 2>/dev/null; exit 0'

bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); SRC=$(jq -r ".source // unknown" <<< "$INPUT" 2>/dev/null); printf "[%s] CONFIG CHANGE: %s\n" "$(date -u +%T)" "$SRC" >> /tmp/cw-session.log 2>/dev/null; echo "Configuration changed ($SRC). Hooks and permissions may have updated."; exit 0'
bash
